description: Analysts dissect reported and guided net yield growth into ticket vs onboard, like-for-like vs new hardware/private destinations, and regional drivers, often quantified in basis points against the FY midpoint.

2022-Q3: ticket vs onboard split going forward
puts and takes vs 2019 baseline (Pullmantur, Azamara, CocoCay, China exit)
2022-Q4: occupancy vs revenue per diem split
2023-Q1: onboard staying healthy vs decelerating in back half
bps quantification of transition cost and expense timing
2023-Q2: stacking 2024 yield tailwinds (Icon, CocoCay expansion, China)
ticket vs onboard mix on leading-edge price
2023-Q3: yield-cost spread in bps (200-400 normal vs ~900 actual)
2023-Q4: yield cadence tied to Caribbean seasonality
discount-to-land closure as multi-year yield driver
2024-Q1: capacity x yield x cost algorithm to $20 EPS
2024-Q2: Q4 implied 5% as deceleration vs tough load-factor comp
2024-Q3: like-for-like vs cumulative US inflation since 2019
PDMx yield uplift parallel to CocoCay for Western Caribbean
2024-Q4: 3.5% midpoint bridge from high-single per diems with 40bp tough comp
river cruise yield premium vs existing fleet
2025-Q2: why top end of yield range came down vs Q1 optimism
2025-Q3: cleansed Q4 exit-rate yield (add back 90bp comp + storms)
ticket vs onboard composition as Royal Beach Clubs ramp
2025-Q4: intra-year yield cadence given Q2 dry-dock
2026-Q1: Europe 200bp Q2/Q3 drag and reversion in 2027
Perfect Day Mexico structural lift for Galveston/Western Caribbean

analyst_affinity.steven wieczynski: Breaks yields into ticket vs onboard and probes whether 'moderate yield growth' tagline still fits; pushes on yield-cost spread in bps and PDMx uplift for 2027.
analyst_affinity.robin farley: Quantifies bps walks (occupancy vs price, dry-dock vs structural, European reversion); compares initial guide to pre-pandemic 3-4% baseline.
analyst_affinity.brandt montour: Bridges midpoint guide to per-diem run-rate; isolates like-for-like inside guide and seasonality of Caribbean-weighted quarters.
analyst_affinity.vince ciepiel: Decomposes growth into like-for-like vs new hardware vs CocoCay/private destination lift; tracks new-hardware contribution by region.
analyst_affinity.lizzie dove: Asks whether moderate yield growth bakes in new-ship and private-island premiums or is like-for-like only; probes cadence ramp.
analyst_affinity.conor cunningham: Frames yield growth as a function of closing the discount-to-land gap and close-in mix from shorter itineraries.
analyst_affinity.matthew boss: Asks for durable multi-year yield drivers in bps relative to pre-pandemic margin baseline.
analyst_affinity.benjamin chaiken: Clarifies whether implied yield ramp is occupancy or revenue-per-day and tests bps headwinds from new-ship ramp.
analyst_affinity.patrick scholes: Verifies apples-to-apples versus 2019 reported baseline before accepting bps figures.
analyst_affinity.sharon zackfia: Asks whether owned destinations shift the ticket vs onboard composition of yield.

default_framing_options: ticket vs onboard split within the implied yield walk
bps decomposition by region (Med vs Western Caribbean vs Caribbean)
guidance bridge to FY midpoint with dry-dock timing vs structural carve-out
